title: Data Quality Dashboard
description: The Data Quality Dashboard (DQD) runs OHDSI's standardised data quality checks across all CDM tables. It evaluates completeness, plausibility, and conformance rules and summarises failures by category and threshold.
tips: A DQD run can take 30–90 minutes on large databases — schedule during off-peak hours.
The Overview scorecard shows Pass/Fail/Error counts; drill into a category to see individual check results.
CDM_SOURCE table failures are often the first sign of an incomplete ETL — check this table first.
Plausibility checks flag biologically implausible values (e.g., age > 150) — investigate before using that field in analyses.
Export results to CSV for documentation or to share with data engineering teams.
